title: astragalus punae
intro: Astragalus punae, a species of flowering plant in the legume family, is native to New Zealand and is also known as the mountain trefoil. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Astragalus punae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and improve respiratory health. Astragalus punae can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
